subject: LU-4017 quota: add setting/getting project id function

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Hi,

 There is a problems for LL_MAXQUOTAS, since osd-zfs still did not support project quota,
if we define LL_MAXQUOTAS as 3 directly, osd-zfs will failed to mount,

Any good ideas to fix this?

Hi Yawei,

    I am not sure but since ZFS did not support project quota, we will redine LL_MAXQUOTAS as 3, what if we use osd-zfs, qmt_pool_prepare() will fail?

Patch Set 3:

Fan Yong

Are you able to answer the question about project quotas for ZFS?

Peter

Patch Set 3:

> I am not sure but since ZFS did not support project quota, we will redine LL_MAXQUOTAS as 3, what if we use osd-zfs, qmt_pool_prepare() will fail?

One possible solution is to pass some parameter via qsd_instance, such as qsd_instance::qsd_maxquotas when call qsd_prepare(). Then inside the quota module, NOT directly use LL_MAXQUOTAS, instead, use the qsd_instance::qsd_maxquotas.

Patch Set 3:

I don't think it's a problem of defining MAXQUOTAS as 3, for zfs which doesn't have project accounting, the corresponding "qqi->qsd_acct_failed" should be set, and zfs osd should be mount successfully without project quota enforcement. If it mount failed, then there must be something wrong in code level, what we need to do is find out the defect and fix it.

Something in this patch or in an earlier patch in this series is causing sanity test 133f to hang. One example of this can be found at https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/2a657714-1541-11e7-b742-5254006e85c2 and, if you look at the MDS 1 console log, you can see:

02:04:39:[ 4099.004984] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 133f: Check for LBUGs/Oopses/unreadable files in /proc ================================ 02:04:33 (1490864673)
02:04:39:[ 4099.898932] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: find /proc/fs/lustre/ /proc/sys/lnet/ /sys/fs/lustre/ /sys/kernel/debug/lustre/ ! -name req_history -exec cat {} \;
02:04:39:
02:04:39:[ 4100.730808] BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at 0000000000000038
02:04:39:[ 4100.730808] IP: [<ffffffffa0c4fa92>] lprocfs_quota_seq_open+0x202/0x330 [lquota]
02:04:39:[ 4100.730808] PGD 7811e067 PUD 5c320067 PMD 0 
02:04:39:[ 4100.730808] Oops: 0000 [#1] SMP 

and the stack trace follows.

One question for this patch.  I see that it is sending/setting the project ID on the inode, but where are the "semantics" of project IDs implemented?  Is this dependent on the back-end ldiskfs project ID code to decide whether the project ID can be set on the file, and when it is inherited?

Patch Set 11:

Yup, it call backend ldiskfs to set project ID.

parents.subject: LU-9278 quota: avoid crash when access failed quota proc file

commit.subject: LU-4017 quota: add setting/getting project id function
commit.message:
LU-4017 quota: add setting/getting project id function

Extend Attr RPC to support project ID attribute, new
ioctl is introduced to get/set project id, it is kept
same ioctl number as VFS, you could use:

lsattr -p <file>
chattr -p <file>

Redefine LL_MAXQUOTAS as 3, change
@qsd_acct_failed into qqi, otherwise osd-zfs
could not use USR/GRP quota normally.

Remove osd_quota_migration(), it only output
some messages in case upgrading from 1.8 which
give us extra efforts for project quota, remove
it indead.

also fix a typo bug in osp_attr_set().

Change-Id: I4b537be9efa29fda730592b6d5e41c6b60fb6c0d
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/26202
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
